differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with secondary Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) and Catastrophic Antiphospholipid Syndrome (CAPS)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to occult malignancy
next_steps: Perform serologic testing for Antiphospholipid Syndrome (Anti-cardiolipin, Anti-beta-2-glycoprotein I, and Lupus anticoagulant)
Perform echocardiography (specifically Transesophageal Echocardiogram) to rule out endocarditis and Libman-Sacks vegetation
Obtain specialized infectious disease testing including leptospirosis PCR and TB workup (AFB smear, mycobacterial culture of CSF and BAL fluid, T-SPOT/Quantiferon)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation if CAPS is strongly suspected and hemorrhage risk is managed
Obtain autoimmune panel (C3, C4, Anti-dsDNA, ENA panel)
Perform bone marrow biopsy to exclude underlying hematologic malignancy or hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH)

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with secondary APS
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) secondary to underlying autoimmune process
Systemic vasculitis with pulmonary-renal syndrome (e.g., GPA or microscopic polyangiitis)
next_steps: Obtain emergent serologic testing for APS (Lupus anticoagulant, anti-cardiolipin, anti-beta-2-glycoprotein I)
Perform Transesophageal Echocardiogram to evaluate for vegetation or intracardiac thrombus
Order HLH diagnostic panel (ferritin, soluble IL-2 receptor, triglycerides, fibrinogen)
Consult Neurology and Neurosurgery for management of subarachnoid hemorrhage and anticoagulation risk/benefit
Obtain autoimmune workup (Anti-dsDNA, C3, C4, ENA panel)
Perform bone marrow biopsy with aspiration for flow cytometry and microbiology
